matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 3 > Dinner > Chicken Fajita Bowl — Berry Boost Plate

node.name: Chicken Fajita Bowl — Berry Boost Plate
node.short_description: Protein-forward fajita bowl with chicken, peppers, onions, rice, and avocado; optional berries boost antioxidants for a Berry Boost.
node.meal_suggestion: Grill or sauté chicken with peppers and onions, serve over cooked rice, and top with sliced avocado. Add a side of berries to boost antioxidants and fiber if desired.
node.nutrition_note: Protein-rich chicken with peppers and onions offers lean protein and fiber; rice provides carbohydrates; avocado adds heart-healthy fats. For extra fiber and antioxidants, consider adding a side of berries (Berry Boost).

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 1 > Dinner > Roast Chicken Dinner — Garden Veg Combo

node.name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Garden Veg Combo
node.short_description: A balanced roast dinner with protein-rich chicken, wholesome quinoa, and colorful garden vegetables.
node.meal_suggestion: Roast chicken thighs (skinless if preferred), cook quinoa, and steam or roast broccoli and carrots. Plate together and finish with a squeeze of lemon and a light drizzle of olive oil for flavor.
node.nutrition_note: Protein from chicken thighs; quinoa adds complete protein and fiber; broccoli and carrots provide vitamins, minerals, and additional fiber. For a leaner option, use skinless thighs.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 4 > Dinner > Vegetable Soup & Bread — Garden Veg Variation

node.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Garden Veg Variation
node.short_description: A plant-forward combination of vegetable soup, whole grain bread, and a side salad for fiber, nutrients, and steady energy.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve 1 cup of vegetable soup with 1 to 2 slices of whole grain bread and a side salad. For extra protein, add beans, lentils, or tofu to the soup or include a protein-rich topping on the salad.
node.nutrition_note: Plant-forward, fiber-rich meal with vegetables, whole grains, and greens. High in dietary fiber and vitamins; provides moderate protein from soup and bread. To keep sodium in check, choose a low-sodium vegetable soup. Optional protein boost (beans, lentils, or tofu) can be added for more protein.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 7 > Dinner > Vegetable Soup & Bread — Citrus Fresh Twist

node.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Citrus Fresh Twist
node.short_description: A light, satisfying trio of vegetable soup, whole-grain bread, and a side salad with a citrus-inspired twist.
node.meal_suggestion: Balanced light lunch option. For extra protein, add chickpeas, lentils, or a dollop of yogurt. To enhance the Citrus Fresh Twist, consider a citrus vinaigrette on the salad.
node.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich from the vegetables and whole-grain bread; provides complex carbs, and vitamins/minerals with low saturated fat. Protein is modest; sodium depends on the soup—opt for a low-sodium option to support heart health. Side salad adds hydration and micronutrients.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 6 > Dinner > Baked Cod Plate — Sesame Twist

node.name: Baked Cod Plate — Sesame Twist
node.short_description: Baked cod with roasted sweet potato and broccoli, finished with lemon for a bright, protein-rich plate.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve with quinoa or brown rice for extra fiber. If desired, garnish with sesame seeds or a light sesame oil drizzle to mirror the twist.
node.nutrition_note: Lean cod provides high-quality protein; sweet potato offers complex carbs and beta-carotene; broccoli adds fiber and micronutrients; lemon contributes vitamin C. Optional sesame seeds/oil can add healthy fats to match the 'Sesame Twist'.
